title: Per-Origin Politeness, Token Timing, Retry Budgets, and Fairness · Go Semantics Case: Retry After Jitter
summary: A shared ticker spaces starts globally, one origin monopolizes slots, Retry-After is ignored, jitter is unbounded, and transient failures retry forever. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go retry after jitter increment in monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chan…
objectives: Parse valid Retry-After and combine with capped exponential backoff and bounded jitter while recording the chosen schedule.
Classify transport and HTTP failures by method safety, response evidence, attempt, elapsed, and crawl budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Build deterministic Go evidence to prove per-origin state, start spacing, retry classes, Retry-After, bounded jitter, and fairness; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse valid Retry-After and combine with capped exponential backoff and bounded jitter while recording the chosen schedule.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Retry After Jitter model · Theory Retry After Jitter, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Random sleeps are sufficient politeness and retry evidence.
